reasoning: Superman releases in July 2025 into a highly competitive box office year. It has to beat out behemoths like 'Avatar: Fire and Ash', 'Zootopia 2', 'Jurassic World Rebirth', and 'A Minecraft Movie'. 'A Minecraft Movie' released in April 2025 and has already surpassed $408 million domestically by mid-May, creating a very high bar for the rest of the year. Historically, recent Superman films (like Man of Steel or Batman v Superman) have grossed roughly $290M–$330M domestically. While James Gunn's reboot could overperform, surpassing a high $450M+ threshold set by Minecraft and competing with Avatar 3 (which could easily do $400M+ in just its last 12 days in December, as Avatar 2 did) makes its chances of being the #1 domestic calendar grosser extremely slim. The market price was at 9% on May 15, and I am adjusting slightly downward to 7% due to the confirmed over-performance of 'A Minecraft Movie' severely limiting the top spot availability.
